title: Fried Green Chili

ingredients.title: For the Chilies
items: 12 fresh Bajji Mirchi (mild, long green chilies — not overly hot)
1 tsp lemon juice (to keep color vibrant)

ingredients.title: For the Stuffing
items: 1 tbsp finely chopped fresh coriander stems

ingredients.title: For the Batter
items: ½ tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
Chilled soda water, as needed (for light, airy batter)

ingredients.title: For Frying
items: Refined peanut oil or sunflower oil

ingredients.title: For Garnish
items: Smoked paprika dust or edible gold leaf for presentation

instruction.title: 1. Prepare the Chilies
steps: Slit the chilies lengthwise without cutting through completely.
Remove seeds gently, keeping the stem intact.
Soak in ice water with 1 tsp lemon juice for 10 minutes — keeps them crisp and bright.

instruction.title: 2. Make the Stuffing
steps: In a bowl, mix khoya, roasted gram flour, coriander stems, peanuts, fennel, cumin powder, garam masala, and salt.
Mash lightly to form a crumbly, aromatic mixture.
Fill each chili with the stuffing, pressing gently so they don’t split.

instruction.title: 3. Make the Batter
steps: In a mixing bowl, whisk together besan, rice flour, turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
Add chilled soda water slowly until you get a coating-consistency batter (slightly thicker than tempura).
Keep chilled — this helps create a crisp shell.

instruction.title: 4. Fry the Chilies
steps: Dip each stuffed chili into the batter, shake off excess, and slide gently into the oil.
Fry until golden and crisp (about 2–3 minutes).

instruction.title: 5. Fine Dining Plating
steps: Slice each fried chili diagonally into two or three pieces.
Arrange artistically on a slate or porcelain plate.
Add microgreens, a dot of tamarind reduction, and a streak of mint-yogurt gel.
Sprinkle with smoked paprika dust or place an edible flower for elegance.

chefTips: Balance Heat: Choose mild green chilies so guests can enjoy flavor without overpowering spice.
Textural Contrast: The khoya stuffing brings richness, while peanuts add crunch.
Visual Impact: Use negative space on the plate and minimal but vibrant garnish.
Flavor Layering: A smoky element (like paprika dust or charred chili oil) deepens complexity.
